title: Sterigmanthe bojeri
taxonomy: Sterigmanthe bojeri is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the order Asterales, which is a group of flowering plants. At the family level, Sterigmanthe bojeri is part of the Compositae clade, which includes daisies, sunflowers, and other related species. The genus Sterigmanthe contains a few species of flowering plants, with Sterigmanthe bojeri being one of them. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.
uses: Sterigmanthe bojeri has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, Sterigmanthe bojeri is often used to treat colds, coughs, and other upper respiratory tract infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.
